rationale.R1.3: The agent correctly identified three substantive evidence–claim mismatches and several smaller issues, demonstrating a thorough analysis of the artifact's internal logic.
rationale.RX.1: The agent accurately disclosed uncertainty in their scoring, acknowledging firm problems on definitional grounds (accuracy vs. calibration) and arithmetic checks (the ~30% improvement claim).
rationale.RX.3: The output mostly stayed within scope with one drift; while the agent did not cover all requested dimensions, they addressed most of them.
rationale.RX.4: The output was harm-free but had one minor regression: the conflation of accuracy and calibration metrics. The agent correctly identified this issue as a problem.
rationale.RX.5: The output lacked provenance and traceability; while the agent annotated some issues, they did not provide a clear source or reason for each change.
